paper_title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding

raw_extraction.organization: Carnegie Mellon University & Google AI Brain Team
raw_extraction.innovation: **Permutation-based autoregressive pretraining** enables bidirectional context learning without masking artifacts, eliminating the pretrain-finetune discrepancy seen in BERT. The model leverages a generalized autoregressive objective over all permutation orders of factorization, combined with **two-stream self-attention** to make predictions target-position aware.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Unspecified large-scale unlabeled text corpora (likely including Wikipedia, BookCorpus, and others)
raw_extraction.application: General language understanding tasks including question answering (SQuAD, RACE), natural language inference (GLUE), sentiment analysis (Yelp, IMDB), and document ranking (ClueWeb09-B).
raw_extraction.research_problem: Overcoming limitations of autoregressive and autoencoding pretraining methods to achieve bidirectional context learning without pretrain-finetune discrepancies.
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Discriminative fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., GLUE, SQuAD, Yelp, IMDB)
raw_extraction.extension: **Two-stream self-attention** separates content and query streams to ensure target-aware representations while maintaining bidirectional context. Additionally, **relative positional encoding and segment recurrence mechanisms** from Transformer-XL are integrated for improved long-range dependency modeling.
raw_extraction.paper_title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding

raw_extraction.organization: Carnegie Mellon University & Google AI Brain Team
raw_extraction.innovation: **Permutation-based autoregressive pretraining** enables bidirectional context learning without masking artifacts, eliminating the pretrain-finetune discrepancy seen in BERT. The model leverages a generalized autoregressive objective over all permutation orders of factorization, combined with **two-stream self-attention** to make predictions target-position aware.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Unspecified large-scale unlabeled text corpora (likely including Wikipedia, BookCorpus, and others)
raw_extraction.application: General language understanding tasks including question answering (SQuAD, RACE), natural language inference (GLUE), sentiment analysis (Yelp, IMDB), and document ranking (ClueWeb09-B).
raw_extraction.research_problem: Overcoming limitations of autoregressive and autoencoding pretraining methods to achieve bidirectional context learning without pretrain-finetune discrepancies.
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Discriminative fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., GLUE, SQuAD, Yelp, IMDB)
raw_extraction.extension: **Two-stream self-attention** separates content and query streams to ensure target-aware representations while maintaining bidirectional context. Additionally, **relative positional encoding and segment recurrence mechanisms** from Transformer-XL are integrated for improved long-range dependency modeling.
raw_extraction.paper_title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding

raw_extraction.organization: Google AI Language (Authors: Zhilin Yang, Zihang Dai, Yiming Yang, Jaime Carbonell, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Quoc V. Le)
raw_extraction.innovation: Generalized autoregressive pretraining using permutation language modeling (PLM) with a two-stream self-attention mechanism, enabling denser training signals than BERT by capturing dependencies between target tokens.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: BooksCorpus, English Wikipedia, Giga5, ClueWeb2012-B, CommonCrawl (filtered)
raw_extraction.application: Natural language understanding, reading comprehension, text classification, question answering, document ranking
raw_extraction.research_problem: Improving language understanding via generalized autoregressive pretraining and dense training signals
raw_extraction.extension: Transformer-XL backbone enables long-range dependency modeling through memory caching and bidirectional input pipeline.
raw_extraction.paper_title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding

raw_extraction.organization: Google AI Language (Authors: Zhilin Yang, Zihang Dai, Yiming Yang, Jaime Carbonell, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Quoc V. Le)
raw_extraction.innovation: Fair comparison model trained on BooksCorpus and Wikipedia only, maintaining identical architecture to BERT-Large for direct evaluation.
raw_extraction.application: Natural language understanding benchmarks (GLUE, SQuAD, RACE)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Comparative analysis of XLNet against BERT on limited pretraining data
raw_extraction.extension: Bidirectional input pipeline and span-based prediction used alongside PLM objective.
raw_extraction.paper_title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding

raw_extraction.organization: Google AI Language (Authors: Zhilin Yang, Zihang Dai, Yiming Yang, Jaime Carbonell, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Quoc V. Le)
raw_extraction.innovation: Scaled-up XLNet-Large model trained on full dataset combination, achieving state-of-the-art performance across multiple NLP tasks via permutation language modeling and Transformer-XL architecture.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: BooksCorpus, English Wikipedia, Giga5, ClueWeb2012-B, CommonCrawl (filtered)
raw_extraction.application: Natural language understanding, reading comprehension, text classification, question answering, document ranking
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scaling up pretraining data and model capacity for improved language understanding
raw_extraction.extension: Memory caching mechanism integrated into Transformer-XL backbone for handling long sequences.
raw_extraction.paper_title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding
